message:
fuzz(ext4-image-reader): 18 structured seeds across all four input modes

fuzz_ext4_image_reader builds inputs in four shapes (clean / mutated /
truncated / raw) and asserts the reader workflow is deterministic for
identical inputs. Existing 3 seeds (clean / mutated / truncated)
covered only the trivial happy paths — mode 3 (raw) had no seed at
all, the mutation count was always 1, and the truncate length was
never near the boundary.

Adds 18 seeds spanning every documented branch:

  Mode 0 (clean):
  * pure clean image, no further cursor reads.

  Mode 1 (clean + mutations) covering all 12 INTERESTING_OFFSETS:
  * single-mutation magic flip (offset[1] = sb+0x38)
  * single-mutation log_block_size zero (offset[0] = sb+0x18)
  * 5-mutation pass over the entire SB region
  * 32-mutation pass at MAX_MUTATIONS (drives the upper bound)
  * single-mutation at a random (non-INTERESTING) offset
  * mutated mode with mutation_count = 0 (mode=1 but no mutations)
  * root-dir block mutation (offsets 8/9 = BLOCK*4 area)
  * inode-table mutations (offsets 5/6/7 = BLOCK*2 + 0x00/0x80/0x100)
  * symlink data block mutations (offsets 10/11 = BLOCK*5/BLOCK*6)

  Mode 2 (truncate) at boundary lengths:
  * truncate to 0 (smallest possible — must reject)
  * truncate to 32 (well below superblock at 1024)
  * truncate to 200 (still below superblock end)
  * truncate to 255 (largest reachable via byte mod IMAGE_SIZE+1)

  Mode 3 (raw — previously unseeded):
  * 64 bytes of zeros (no magic — parser rejects)
  * 1088 bytes carrying the ext4 magic at sb+0x38 (otherwise zero)
  * 4 KiB of zeros (one block worth, no magic)
  * 16 KiB of 0xFF (full-image-shaped garbage)

All 21 corpus inputs load cleanly with `-runs=0`; coverage rises
to 726 PCs / 829 features.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
